title: Blinking Two LEDs
subtitle: Current Sourcing vs. Current Sinking
overview: In this session, we will learn the difference between current sourcing and current sinking by revisiting our LED Blink example. We will build two simple LED circuits using a breadboard to see how driving an Arduino pin HIGH can turn one LED on, but turn another LED off!

images._note: Add photos from this session — Arduino board, LED circuits.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Photo of Arduino UNO board connected to two LEDs on a breadboard
gallery.caption: Two LEDs blinking alternately

keyTopics: Current Sourcing: Driving current OUT of the pin to GND (Pin is HIGH = LED ON)
Current Sinking: Allowing current to flow INTO the pin from 5V (Pin is LOW = LED ON)
Wiring circuits cleanly using a breadboard
Writing Arduino code to control multiple LEDs with digitalWrite()

youtubeVideos.title: Breadboarding Circuits
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how to use breadboards to create clean, organized circuits.

resources.title: Physical Computing L6: Blinking Two LEDs

activity.title: Build Two LED Circuits
steps: Step 1: Wire up the power and GND rails on the breadboard
Step 2: Wire up LED Circuit 1 (Anode to Pin 3, Cathode to GND via resistor)
Step 3: Wire up LED Circuit 2 (Anode to 5V via resistor, Cathode to Pin 4)
Step 4: Write the setup code to set Pins 3 and 4 as OUTPUT
Step 5: Write the loop code with digitalWrite(3, HIGH) and digitalWrite(4, HIGH)
Step 6: Upload and see how the LEDs alternate even with the exact same code values!

quiz.q: What happens when you drive Pin 4 HIGH in LED Circuit 2 (where anode is connected to 5V)?
quiz.explanation: When Pin 4 is HIGH (5V), there is no voltage difference across the circuit (from Pin 4 to 5V), so no current flows and the LED turns off.

options: Current flows from a 5V source into an Arduino pin
Driving current out of an Arduino pin to GND
Using a battery instead of USB
quiz.explanation: Current sourcing is when an Arduino GPIO pin acts as a source, driving current out of the pin to GND.

options: When an LED falls into a liquid
Driving current out of an Arduino pin to GND
Allowing current to flow into the Arduino pin from a higher voltage source
quiz.explanation: Current sinking is when a pin allows current to flow INTO it from a higher voltage source (like 5V).

quiz.q: Why do we use a breadboard?
options: To provide power to the Arduino
To make it easier to make a clean, organized, and temporary circuit
quiz.explanation: Breadboards allow you to temporarily plug in components and wires to prototype circuits without soldering.
